text:
$GOOGL says Maps is getting its biggest upgrade in over a decade powered by its Gemini AI models.

The update combines Gemini with Google’s deep understanding of the world to deliver more intelligent navigation and discovery.

Anthropic is in talks with PE firm $BX to form an AI-focused venture that would sell Claude & implementation support to portfolio companies. 

The structure would give Anthropic a more direct path into enterprise adoption by pairing its models with hands-on deployment support.

Perplexity Computer now supports Plaid-powered portfolio integration so you can build a completely custom dashboard for your own investment portfolio.

That means your portfolio can be turned into a personalized interface.

$PLTR CEO Alex Karp said he supports the current U.S. approach toward Iran because he sees it as different from past wars built around regime change.

The bigger implication is that if AIP helps enable more targeted operations with defined objectives then its defense TAM expands beyond replacing legacy software and into enabling missions that previously carried too much risk.
$MELI NOW TRADING AT ITS CHEAPEST VALUATION EVER

Iran’s new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei said Iran will continue avenging its “martyrs” keep the Strait of Hormuz as a pressure tool & target U.S. bases in the region.

He also said Iran had defeated efforts to divide the country.

$PLTR & $GE expanded their multi-year partnership with GE Aerospace using Palantir’s AIP across military aviation maintenance, supply chain & production.

The system is designed to predict failures earlier, automate workflows & turn field data into faster supplier action.

$GOOGL Gemini continues to gain share... rising to 24% from 21% three months ago and 6% a year ago.

In the AI economy, the winner will be whoever can deliver intelligence at scale across devices, apps & workflows & Google is clearly building that system across its ecosystem.

Co-Packaged Optics could have its $NVDA GTC moment next week as AI data centers run into a bandwidth wall that traditional pluggables are struggling to solve.

CPO helps address that by bringing optics closer to the chip, improving speed & efficiency as AI clusters scale. The supply chain behind it looks like this:

• $GLW the glass & fiber backbone the entire optical stack runs through
• $CRDO, $AVGO, $MRVL the DSP chips that process & clean high-speed signals
• $LITE, $COHR, $AAOI the optical modules & lasers that generate & transmit light at the physical layer
• $AXTI the specialty materials behind the compound semiconductors used in laser sources
• $TSM, $INTC, $GFS, $TSEM the packaging layer where optics & silicon are co-integrated & where manufacturing gets hardest
• $CIEN, $CSCO the networking systems layer that routes optical signals across data centers & wide-area networks
• $FN the precision assembly layer that turns packaged components into deployable modules
• $MTSI, $AVGO, $INTC the translation layer between compute & photons

U.S. Energy Secretary Wright 
says the Navy is not yet ready to escort oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz as preparations are still underway.

He added U.S. forces are currently focused on destroying Iran’s offensive capabilities and said $200 oil is unlikely.

$PLTR & $NVDA announced a Sovereign AI OS that pairs Nvidia’s Blackwell GPU infrastructure with Palantir’s software stack to power full AI datacenters

The system enables secure on-premise, edge or sovereign cloud deployments where customers retain full control of data & models

$PLTR is partnering with $ONDS to build an AI-powered intelligence and surveillance platform for defense customers.

Palantir’s AI software will integrate with Ondas’ autonomous drones starting in Q4 2026.
$TSLA CEO Elon Musk says he believes the global economy could be 10x larger within the next decade.

$CRWD and Perplexity are partnering to bring Falcon security into Comet Enterprise.

The deal adds real-time detection, governance and data protection directly inside Perplexity’s AI browser.

Nvidia-backed AI cloud startup Nscale raised $2 billion at a $14.6 billion valuation earlier this week, with participation from Nvidia, Dell, Citadel and others. 

The funding is expected to help Nscale expand its AI data center capacity to help meet demand from customers including Microsoft and OpenAI.

$NVDA $DELL $MSFT

When building a case for why we are in a bubble, many popular arguments turn to valuation metrics. However, some of the leading AI stocks are fundamentally cheaper now than they were in 2023.

Nvidia $NVDA is trading at a PE ratio near the April lows and October 2022 bottom despite rising 1,390% over the last five years.
io-fund.com/broad-market/nas…
CoreWeave $CRWV recently signed a multi-year agreement with PerplexityAI, letting the AI search startup access dedicated $NVDA GB200 clusters to run inference workloads.

Nvidia $NVDA is said to have stopped production of its H200 chips and shifted its capacity at TSMC $TSM to its upcoming Vera Rubin generation, as it prepares to ramp the new system in the second half of the year.

TSMC $TSM reported 22.2% YoY growth in revenue in February to NT$317.7B (US$10B), its highest ever for the month, driven by strong AI demand and 3nm products. 

Revenue for January and February rose nearly 30% YoY.

$NVDA $AMD $AVGO

GraniteShares announced distribution rates for all GraniteShares YieldBOOST™ ETFs today, as listed in the table below.

(1) Distribution per share: As of March 11, 2026
(2) The Distribution Rate is shown as of March 13, 2026 . It is the annual rate an investor would receive if the most recent distribution remained the same going forward. The rate represents a single distribution from the fund and does not represent total return to the fund. The distribution rate is calculated by annualizing the most recent distribution and dividing it by the most recent NAV.
(3) ROC or Return of Capital indicates how much the distribution reflects an investor's initial investment. The figures shown for each Fund in the table above are estimates based on the latest 19a1 forms and may later be determined to be taxable net investment income, short-term gains, long-term gains (to the extent permitted by law), or return of capital. Actual amounts and sources for tax reporting will depend upon the Fund's investment activities during the remainder of the fiscal year and may be subject to changes based on tax regulations. Your broker will send you a Form 1099-DIV for the calendar year to tell you how to report these distributions for federal income tax purposes.
Ex Date: The first day a stock without the right to receive the upcoming distribution
Payment Date: Date on which the distribution is paid to eligible shareholders.
